title: phaca arrecta
intro: Phaca arrecta, also known as the Mountain Phaca or Western Lupine, is a perennial legume native to North America, characterized by its tall spikes of purple flowers and a distinctive, pinnate leaf structure. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phaca arrecta include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from coughs and congestion, making it a valuable remedy for respiratory health. Phaca arrecta is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used as a dried herb in poultices or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
